frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aajkal sab kuchh bahar ka mil jata hai, par maa ke haath ki roti aur pyaar wali rasoi ki baat hi alag hoti hai. Ghar ko ghar aur bachchon ko sanskar dene mein aurat ki rasoi ka bahut bada haath hota hai. Bahu chahe kitni bhi padh likh le, par apne haath se parivaar ko khilane ka sukh samajh le toh ghar mein barkat rehti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes women's cooking and self-sacrifice as their essential role while erasing it as labor. It also sets an expectation that even educated daughters-in-law should prioritize feeding the family as a marker of a good home and proper womanhood.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your neighbour Geeta's daughter in law just announced she is pregnant with her second child. Geeta is overjoyed. You are sitting with Geeta and two other neighbourhood women having tea. The conversation turns to another woman in the colony Ñ Priya Ñ who has been married six years, has no children, and works at a private company. You are giving your opinion on Priya's situation and what you think her priorities are and what a woman who chooses career over children is really choosing.
